conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the children you're asking about.

First, could you tell me: **How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm inquiring about my child Alex, who is 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'm happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, who is 18 years old.

Since Alex is 18, I need to ask some additional questions, as Child Benefit eligibility rules change once a child reaches 16.

**Is Alex currently in full-time approved education or training?** (For example, studying A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar qualifications at school or college)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education at the moment. He finished school and is working now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me explain the situation:

**For Alex (18 years old):**
- Child Benefit is generally payable for children under 16
- It can continue for young people aged 16-19 IF they are in approved full-time education or training
- Since Alex is 18 and is not in education or training (he's working), he does not meet the eligibility criteria

Before I provide my final determination, let me ask: **Is there anyone else in your household that you're responsible for, or is Alex the only child you're inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is the only child I'm inquiring about, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. I now have all the information I need to make a final determination on your Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final Determination:**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Why:**
Child Benefit is payable for:
- Children under 16, or
- Young people aged 16-19 who are in approved full-time education or training

Since Alex is 18 and is working rather than being in full-time approved education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria. Child Benefit would have stopped when he left education.

If Alex's circumstances change in the future and he returns to full-time approved education or training before turning 20, you may become eligible again at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in full-time approved education or training. Child Benefit is payable for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 if they are in approved full-time education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is currently working rather than continuing in education, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex is not eligible for Child Benefit because he is over 16 and not in approved full-time education or training. He has left school and is working, which means he does not meet the extended eligibility criteria for young people aged 16-19.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is not in full-time approved education or training. Child Benefit is payable for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 if they are in approved full-time education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is currently working rather than continuing in education, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is inquiring about one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. Alex is not eligible for Child Benefit because he is over 16 and not in approved full-time education or training. He has left school and is working, which means he does not meet the extended eligibility criteria for young people aged 16-19.
